Vincent: The counter was clean before closing.

Lena: It is not clean now.

Vienne: It never stayed clean for long.

Lena: Someone asked about Entry 0001 again.

Vincent: What did you tell them?

Lena: Nothing that helped.

Vienne: A new page appeared in the archive.

Vincent: Was it written by us?

Vienne: Not according to the record.

Vincent: The lights flickered at the same time again.

Lena: That is the third time this week.

Vienne: It will not be the last.

Lena: The locked door was open for a moment.

Vincent: That is not possible.

Vienne: It still happened.

Vincent: Someone left a message on the counter.

Lena: What did it say?

Vincent: It said nothing we could read.

Vienne: The archive is missing a section.

Vincent: Missing or removed?

Vienne: That depends on when you look at it.

Lena: A guest said they heard footsteps upstairs.

Vincent: There is no upstairs.

Vienne: There was not supposed to be.

Vincent: The guestbook opened on its own.

Lena: To which page?

Vincent: The first one.

Vienne: It always returns there.

Vienne: Three entries now reference the same dream.

Vincent: Coincidence?

Vienne: Patterns do not repeat by accident.

Lena: Someone recognized me from before.

Vincent: From where?

Lena: That is what I asked them.

Vincent: The radio turned on again.

Lena: What was playing?

Vincent: Static with a rhythm.

Vienne: The archive recorded a night that has not happened yet.

Vincent: That is impossible.

Vienne: It is still recorded.

Lena: The chairs keep changing positions overnight.

Vincent: Are you moving them?

Lena: I am not.

Vienne: An entry has no author.

Vincent: That is normal.

Vienne: Not this one.

Vincent: The hallway felt longer tonight.

Lena: There is no hallway.

Vincent: I know.

Vienne: A page was removed and returned twice.

Vincent: By who?

Vienne: That is not recorded.

Lena: Someone asked if we sleep.

Vincent: What did you answer?

Lena: I said yes.

Vienne: That was incorrect.

Vincent: The bar felt full but no one arrived.

Lena: How is that possible?

Vienne: It is not.

Vienne: The archive has begun duplicating entries.

Vincent: Identical?

Vienne: Word for word.

Lena: A customer said they had been here before it opened.

Vincent: That makes no sense.

Vienne: That is what they all say.

Vincent: The clock stopped again.

Lena: At the same time?

Vincent: Exactly.

Vienne: As expected.

Vienne: Someone edited an old entry.

Vincent: Who gave permission?

Vienne: No one.

Lena: The door opened by itself after closing.

Vincent: Was anyone there?

Lena: Not visibly.

Vienne: The archive is heavier tonight.

Vincent: That is not measurable.

Vienne: It is noticeable.

Vincent: A glass appeared on the counter.

Lena: Was it used?

Vincent: No.

Vienne: Not yet.

Lena: The same name appeared twice in different entries.

Vincent: Same person?

Vienne: Different nights.

Vienne: Entry 4021 describes tomorrow.

Vincent: That cannot be real.

Vienne: And yet it exists.

Vincent: Someone knocked after closing.

Lena: Did you open the door?

Vincent: There was no one there.

Vienne: There rarely is.

Lena: The archive feels wrong tonight.

Vincent: Wrong how?

Lena: Like it is watching us.

Vienne: It always has been.

Vienne: A record appeared without a source.

Vincent: That should not happen.

Vienne: It already did.

Vincent: The guestbook is missing pages.

Lena: How many?

Vienne: The same number as yesterday.

Lena: Someone said they heard their name inside the bar.

Vincent: Was anyone calling them?

Lena: No one we could see.

Vienne: The archive is writing faster than we are.

Vincent: That is impossible.

Vienne: And yet.

Vincent: The lights dimmed when no one touched them.

Lena: That keeps happening.

Vienne: It is increasing.

Lena: A guest asked what is behind the door.

Vincent: Which door?

Lena: The one we do not talk about.

Vienne: Correct answer.

Vienne: An entry describes this conversation before it happened.

Vincent: That cannot be true.

Vienne: It is still there.

Vincent: The archive is missing its beginning.

Lena: Everything has a beginning.

Vienne: Not everything remains.

Lena: Someone said they dreamed this place.

Vincent: Did they?

Lena: Too many details matched.

Vienne: That is why it matters.

Vienne: Another page has gone blank.

Vincent: Permanently?

Vienne: We assume so.

Vincent: The bar feels different after midnight.

Lena: It always does.

Vienne: Not always the same way.

Lena: The archive keeps repeating itself.

Vincent: That is not possible.

Vienne: And yet it continues.
